unlikely_phrases.message: The phrase "character school" is grammatically possible, but it sounds semantically unclear in everyday English.
unlikely_phrases.suggestion: Use a clearer noun phrase or remove the unusual modifier.

unlikely_phrases.message: The common collocation is "do homework", not "make homework".
The common collocation is "make a mistake", not "do a mistake".
English usually says "heavy rain", not "strong rain".
English usually says "heavy rain", not "big rain".
"eat water" sounds semantically odd. English usually uses "drink water".
"drink bread" sounds semantically odd. English usually uses "eat bread".
After "by", this phrase needs a more natural adverb/verb form. English would more naturally say "covertly encouraging".
"bully" names a person, but this context needs the action noun "bullying".
"unquestionable" sounds positive here. A more natural critical adjective is "objectionable" or "inappropriate".
"effect" is usually a noun here. The sentence needs the verb "affect".
"designed on the wall" sounds unnatural for posters. English more naturally says "put up on the wall".
unlikely_phrases.suggestion: Use "put up on the wall" instead.

go_to.message: "go to" usually points to a destination or place, but "{object}" does not look like one here.
go_to.suggestion: Use a place noun after "go to", such as "school", "work", or "the park".

eat.message: "eat" usually takes food nouns, but "{object}" sounds odd here.
eat.suggestion: Use a food noun after "eat", or switch to a verb that matches the object.

drink.message: "drink" usually takes liquids or beverages, but "{object}" sounds odd here.
drink.suggestion: Use a beverage noun after "drink", or switch to a verb that matches the object.

read.message: "read" usually takes text or document nouns, but "{object}" sounds odd here.
read.suggestion: Use a reading-related object after "read", or switch to another verb.

watch.message: "watch" usually takes something visual, but "{object}" sounds odd here.
watch.suggestion: Use a visual object after "watch", or switch to another verb.

wear.message: "wear" usually takes clothes or accessories, but "{object}" sounds odd here.
wear.suggestion: Use a clothing-related object after "wear", or switch to another verb.

drive.message: "drive" usually takes a vehicle, but "{object}" sounds odd here.
drive.suggestion: Use a vehicle noun after "drive", or switch to another verb.

ride.message: "ride" usually takes an animal or rideable vehicle, but "{object}" sounds odd here.
ride.suggestion: Use a rideable object after "ride", or switch to another verb.

school.message: The phrase "{modifier} school" sounds unusual in this context.
school.suggestion: Use a common school type such as "high school" or just "school".

rain.message: The phrase "{modifier} rain" sounds unusual in this context.
rain.suggestion: Use a common collocation such as "heavy rain" or "light rain".

coverage_matrix.label: be verbs and basic sentence patterns
have, past forms, and perfect aspect
verb endings and third-person singular agreement
word order and basic clause structure

feature_catalog.description: Exercises that target simple present verb forms.
Exercises that target simple past verb forms.
Exercises that target simple future verb forms.
Exercises that target present continuous verb forms.
Exercises that focus on choosing the correct verb form for a subject.
Exercises that focus on object pronoun choice.
Exercises that focus on verb and preposition combinations.
Exercises that focus on basic subject-verb-object completeness.

past perfect continuous: for a long time before he quit

missing_subject.prompt: ____ built a new house last year.

missing_verb.prompt: My brother ____ to the museum yesterday.
The chef ____ a delicious meal tonight.

subject-verb agreement.description: Use the base verb instead of the third-person singular form.
Add an unnecessary -s to a plural subject.

object pronoun.description: Use the subject pronoun instead of the object pronoun.

verb-preposition.description: Replace the correct preposition with a common learner error.

preprocessing_steps: load raw lexical packs from preprocessing/raw
normalize rule specs and deduplicate tokens
expand irregular and regular verb paradigms
compile synonym graph and semantic classes
build phrase index for time markers and collocations
compile exercise blueprints, lexical pools, and realization rules
merge external common-word lexicon into dictionary
emit compiled knowledge base and pipeline report

sources.description: Time expressions, grammar rules, semantic heuristics, and coverage matrix.
Curated irregular verb paradigms used by the tense and lookup engines.
Core regular-verb seed list expanded during preprocessing.
Synonym clusters normalized into a bidirectional lookup graph.
Semantic noun classes used to validate destinations, objects, and collocations.
Exercise blueprints, lexical pools, morphology rules, and feature catalogs for DSL-driven quiz generation.
Top English words merged into the runtime spelling dictionary during compilation.
Imported SCOWL word list snapshot normalized for spelling and dictionary enrichment.
